item.title: China’s Tax Crackdown Jolts Metal Traders in Financial Hub
item.description: A sweeping crackdown by China’s tax authorities is rattling traders in the world’s biggest metals market, with many firms grappling with severe reductions in invoicing quotas that underpin their trading activities.

item.title: Colombia’s Cepeda Seen Defeating Conservative Rivals in Runoff
item.description: Colombian presidential candidate Iván Cepeda consolidated his lead in the most recent poll and would defeat both conservative rivals in a runoff scenario, five weeks ahead of the vote.

item.title: Goldman Hikes Oil Price Forecasts on ‘Extreme’ Inventory Draws
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. lifted its oil-price forecasts as the prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz spurs “extreme” inventory draws.

item.title: China Delays Foreign Debt Sales With $100 Billion of Bonds Due
item.description: China is tightening approvals for overseas borrowings, leading some companies to scurry for cash with around $100 billion of bonds coming due this year.

item.title: Keyence Jumps 16% to Daily Limit on Earnings Beat, Buyback Plan
item.description: Keyence Corp. shares surged 16%, the biggest gain since August 2024, after the Japanese maker of factory automation sensors delivered quarterly operating income that exceeded analyst estimates and said it will seek investor approval to amend its charter to enable buybacks.

item.title: India Tech Giants Struggle to Shake Off $115 Billion Rout
item.description: Earnings from India’s bellwether software services exporters have reinforced investor concerns about the sector’s growth prospects, signaling that the downturn in their stocks has further to run.

item.title: Sun Pharma to Acquire US-Listed Organon in $12 Billion Deal
item.description: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. agreed to acquire New York-listed women’s health-care company Organon & Co., in what is likely to be one of the biggest India outbound deals in years.

item.title: Nomura Shares Fall After Quarterly Earnings Miss Estimates
item.description: Nomura Holdings Inc. shares fell after its fourth-quarter profit missed analysts’ estimates, due to writedowns and a loss in Europe.

item.title: Rohm Shares Drop 16% on Fears of Denso Scrapping Takeover Bid
item.description: Rohm Co. shares fell as much as 16% after Denso Corp. said it’s considering withdrawing a proposal to take over the power chip maker, dashing hopes for a quick consolidation of Japan’s fragmented supply chain.

item.title: AI Boom Drowns Out War Fears to Fuel Asia’s Great Market Divide
item.description: Asia’s markets are behaving as though two different worlds exist at once.

item.title: Thai Baht War Losses Set to Deepen on Oil Shock, Strategists Say
item.description: The Thai baht has been among the worst performers in Asia since the start of the Iran War and it’s at risk of further losses as the conflict drives up oil prices, strategists say.

item.title: BOJ Seen Holding Rates in Messaging Risk for Ueda as Yen Teeters
item.description: The Bank of Japan is widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged on Tuesday, setting up a communication challenge for Governor Kazuo Ueda as the foundering yen hovers near levels that have prompted past interventions.

item.title: Warner Bros. Approved the Paramount Deal. Here’s What Comes Next.
item.description: The company’s streaming service has lost more than $11 billion and can’t keep customers form canceling

item.title: Asia’s Busiest Earnings Week May Offer Early Taste of War Impact
item.description: Asian companies are heading into the busiest week of the current earnings season, offering investors an early glimpse of how the Iran war has affected financial results.

item.title: Economists Rethink Chinese Forecasts as AI Fires Up Import Surge
item.description: Economists have sharply upgraded their forecasts for China’s import growth and now expect it to overtake the pace of expansion in exports for the first time since 2021, keeping the trade balance from ballooning much beyond last year’s record.

item.title: Gold Declines as Attempts to Restart US-Iran Peace Talks Falter
item.description: Gold fell after efforts to resume peace talks between the US and Iran stalled and energy flows via the Strait of Hormuz remained choked, two months into a war that’s upended global markets and raised inflation risks.

item.title: US Stock Futures Dip as Iran Talks Stall, Oil Up: Markets Wrap
item.description: US equity-index futures dropped and crude oil rose after peace talks between the US and Iran stalled, prolonging the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Oil Rises as Hormuz Stays Shut for Third Month After Talks Stall
item.description: Oil gained after efforts to resume peace talks over the Iran war stalled, with the vital Strait of Hormuz remaining effectively closed, extending disruptions in the Middle East that have roiled global markets.

item.title: Goldman, JPMorgan Show Wall Street’s Split in Quantum Computing Race
item.description: As a breakthrough proves elusive in the quest to deploy the nascent technology and boost earnings, global finance is divided on how to proceed.

item.title: Record-Setting Momentum Rally Is Drawing Doubters
item.description: April’s rally in risk assets has weathered stalled peace talks, signs of resurgent inflation and a central-bank succession drama. Investors may be about to find out if the speed of the advance itself poses a threat to a further ascent.

item.title: Bond Traders Await Powell Update, Slate of US Treasury Auctions
item.description: Traders in the US Treasury market are turning their focus to this week’s Federal Reserve meeting to gauge how officials are assessing the outlook for inflation as tensions in the Middle East keep oil prices elevated.

item.title: Bond Traders Eye Packed Week of Rate Decisions for Sell Signals
item.description: The world’s most important central banks will potentially hand investors fresh reasons to sell government bonds this week as policymakers find themselves forced to confront the risk of a war-driven inflation shock.

item.title: Canadian Province of Manitoba Says It Will Ban Social Media, AI For Youth
item.description: The Canadian province of Manitoba plans to prohibit young people from accessing social media and artificial intelligence chatbots, its leader said.

item.title: Italy Decides to Extradite Chinese Man Wanted by US for Hacking
item.description: The Italian government decided to extradite a Chinese man wanted by the US on hacking charges, people familiar with the matter said on Sunday.

item.title: Raízen Adds Cash in Offer to Creditors, Balks at Board Changes
item.description: Raízen SA sent an alternative proposal to creditors as it tries to iron out terms of a 65 billion-real ($13 billion) debt restructuring, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Apple’s Cook Gives Ternus a Pipeline of 10 Major New Product Categories
item.description: Also: John Ternus talks AI and services with employees.

item.title: Investors Protecting Stocks at Record Eye Bets on Higher Rates
item.description: As stocks hit record after record, some investors are taking to heart the saying: Hedge when you can, not when you must.

item.title: Warsh Confirmation Set to Advance as Key GOP Holdout Backs Vote
item.description: Senator Thom Tillis said he’s dropping his blockade of Kevin Warsh’s nomination to head the Federal Reserve after the Justice Department ended a criminal probe targeting Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

item.title: Big Tech’s $16 Trillion Earnings Week Is Make-Or-Break for Rally
item.description: Wall Street’s biggest technology stocks have carried the S&P 500 to record highs even as the war in Iran continues. Now, earnings from a handful of them this week will give investors a read on whether this rally’s sustainable.

item.title: Israel President Holds Off Deciding Whether to Pardon Netanyahu
item.description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog has decided to shelve, for now, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s request for a pardon in his graft trial, preferring to press the parties to reach an agreement that would terminate the case consensually, his office announced.
